name: agent_close
description: Close a child sub-agent session by cancelling it if still running. Returns the final session projection with transcript_handle metadata.

name: agent_eval
description: Fetch or wait on a child sub-agent session. Optionally deliver a message/items to a running session, then return the latest session projection. With block=true (default), waits for the session to reach a terminal boundary; block=false is a non-blocking status fetch. Terminal projections expose a handle_read-compatible transcript_handle for the full child transcript.

agent_id.description: Generated agent id returned by agent_open

message.description: Optional message to deliver before evaluating the session

items.description: Structured input items (text, mention, skill, local_image, image)

interrupt.description: When sending input, prioritize it over pending inputs

block.description: Wait for a terminal boundary before returning (default true)

timeout_ms.description: Max wait time in milliseconds (default: 30000, clamped to 1000-3600000)

name: agent_open
description:
Open a named child sub-agent session for focused background work. Returns the session name, status, agent_id, context_mode, prefix_cache metadata, and a handle_read-compatible transcript_handle. Use agent_eval to fetch or wait on the session, and agent_close to cancel/close it.

Context control is explicit: omit fork_context or set it false for a fresh child with an independent prefill; set fork_context=true for perspective fanout over the current parent context. Forked children preserve the parent system prompt and leading message prefix byte-identically where the runtime has that prefix, so DeepSeek can reuse its prefix cache before the child-specific task is appended.

Sub-agent results are self-reports. Re-verify claimed side effects such as file edits, commands, network writes, tests, or git operations before reporting them as facts.

name.description: Stable model-facing session name. Defaults to the generated agent_id when omitted.

prompt.description: Initial task description for the child session

items.description: Structured input items (text, mention, skill, local_image, image)

type.description: Sub-agent type: general, explore, plan, review, implementer, verifier, custom

role.description: Role alias: worker, explorer, awaiter, default

allowed_tools.description: Explicit tool allowlist (required for custom type)

model.description: Optional DeepSeek model id for this child

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the child; must be inside the parent workspace

resident_file.description: Optional file path for cache-aware resident mode

fork_context.description: false (default): fresh child with independent context/prefill. true: forked child that preserves the parent's byte-identical system/message prefix where available, then appends this task for DeepSeek prefix-cache reuse.

max_depth.description: Recursive child-agent budget for this session. 0 blocks agent_open from the child; 1-3 allow that many descendant levels.

name: apply_patch
description: Apply a unified-diff patch (multi-hunk, multi-file). Use this instead of `git apply`, `patch`, or repeated `edit_file` calls in `exec_shell` — single transactional change with fuzzy matching and a rendered diff.

path.description: Path to the file to patch (relative to workspace)

fuzz.description: Maximum fuzz factor for fuzzy matching (default: 3)

create_if_missing.description: Create the file if it doesn't exist (for new file patches)

name: automation_create
description: Create a durable scheduled automation. Creation requires approval and recurrence is constrained to supported HOURLY/WEEKLY RRULE forms. Runs enqueue normal durable tasks.

name: automation_delete
description: Delete a durable automation and its run history. Requires approval.

name: automation_list
description: List durable automations with status, next run, and last run timestamps.

name: automation_pause
description: Pause a durable automation. Requires approval.

name: automation_read
description: Read one durable automation plus recent run records.

name: automation_resume
description: Resume a paused durable automation. Requires approval.

name: automation_run
description: Run an automation now. The run enqueues a normal durable task and returns linked task/thread/turn ids as they become available.

name: automation_update
description: Update a durable automation. Requires approval; recurrence remains constrained to supported RRULE forms.

name: checklist_add
description: Add one checklist item on the active thread/task. Durable tasks persist this checklist as subordinate work progress.

name: checklist_list
description: List current checklist progress for the active thread/task.

name: checklist_update
description: Update one checklist item's status by id on the active thread/task.

name: checklist_write
description: Replace the active thread/task checklist. Use this for granular progress under the current durable task or runtime thread; durable tasks remain the real executable work object.

todos.description: The complete list of todo items. This replaces the existing list.

name: diagnostics
description: Report workspace info, git detection, sandbox availability, and Rust toolchain versions.

name: edit_file
description: Replace text in a single file via exact search/replace. Use this instead of `sed -i` in `exec_shell` for one unambiguous in-place edit. `search` matches exactly by default, including whitespace and indentation; set `fuzz: true` to tolerate leading-indentation differences. Returns a compact unified diff, not the full file. For structural, multi-block, or cross-file changes, use `apply_patch` or `write_file` instead.

search.description: Exact text to search for, including whitespace, indentation, and newlines

fuzz.description: When true, tolerate leading whitespace differences on each searched line (default false)

name: exec_interact
description: Send input to a background shell task and return incremental output.

input.description: Input to send to the task's stdin

timeout_ms.description: Wait for output after sending input (default: 1000)

name: exec_shell
description: Execute a shell command in the workspace directory. Foreground mode is for bounded commands; use background=true or task_shell_start for long-running work, then poll/wait.

background.description: Run in background and return task_id (default: false). Prefer true for commands that may run for minutes; poll with exec_shell_wait or task_shell_wait.

interactive.description: Run interactively with terminal IO (default: false)

stdin.description: Optional stdin data to send before waiting (non-interactive only)

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the command

tty.description: Allocate a pseudo-terminal for interactive programs (implies background)

combined_output.description: Capture stdout and stderr as one chronological PTY stream (default false). In foreground mode, waits for completion; in background mode, implies tty.

name: exec_shell_cancel
description: Cancel a running background shell task by task_id, or cancel all running background shell tasks with all=true.

task_id.description: Task ID returned by exec_shell or task_shell_start

all.description: Cancel all currently running background shell tasks

name: exec_shell_interact
description: Send input to a background shell task and return incremental output.

input.description: Input to send to the task's stdin

timeout_ms.description: Wait for output after sending input (default: 1000)

name: exec_shell_wait
description: Wait for a background shell task and return incremental output. Turn cancellation stops waiting but leaves the background task running.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000, max: 600000). Use a higher value for long-running builds, CI watchers, and interactive commands that are expected to keep producing output.

wait.description: Wait for completion before returning (default: true)

name: exec_wait
description: Wait for a background shell task and return incremental output. Turn cancellation stops waiting but leaves the background task running.

timeout_ms.description: Timeout in milliseconds (default: 30000, max: 600000). Use a higher value for long-running builds, CI watchers, and interactive commands that are expected to keep producing output.

wait.description: Wait for completion before returning (default: true)

name: fetch_url
description: Fetch a known URL directly (HTTP GET) and return its content. Use this instead of `curl` in `exec_shell` — sandboxed, network-policy aware, and properly decoded. Plain-text endpoints (`.md`, `.txt`, `.json`, `.yaml`, `raw.githubusercontent.com`, public APIs) prefer this over the browser/automation stack. For unknown queries, use `web_search` first.

format.description: Post-processing for the response body. `markdown` (default) and `text` strip HTML tags to readable text; `raw` returns the body bytes as-is.

max_bytes.description: Truncate response body after this many bytes (default 1,000,000; hard max 10,485,760).

fields.description: Optional JSONPath projections for JSON responses. Supports $, .field, [index], [*], and ['field']; returns matches under `fields`.

name: file_search
description: Find files by name using fuzzy matching with score-based ranking. Use this instead of `find -name` or `fd` in `exec_shell` for filename search. Pass `extensions` to filter by suffix.

query.description: Search query (file name or path fragment).

path.description: Optional base path to search (relative to workspace).

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 20).

extensions.description: Optional list of file extensions to include (e.g. ["rs", "md"]).

exclude.description: Optional glob patterns to exclude, matching grep_files' convention (e.g. ["target/**", "*.lock"]).

name: fim_edit
description: Edit a file using Fill-in-the-Middle (FIM) completion. Provide a file path, prefix_anchor (text that appears before the section to replace), and suffix_anchor (text that appears after the section to replace). The tool calls DeepSeek's FIM endpoint to generate replacement content.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative to workspace)

prefix_anchor.description: Text anchor marking the end of the prefix. Everything up to and including this anchor is kept as-is before the generated middle.

suffix_anchor.description: Text anchor marking the start of the suffix. Everything from this anchor onward is kept as-is after the generated middle.

name: finance
description: Fetch a live market quote for a stock, ETF, or crypto ticker using Yahoo Finance-style public endpoints.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol to look up (for example: AAPL, SPY, BTC).

type.description: Optional asset type hint such as equity, fund, crypto, or index.

market.description: Optional market hint retained for compatibility with finance-style tool calls.

name: git_blame
description: Run `git blame` on a file with optional revision and line-range controls.

path.description: Path to a tracked file within the workspace.

rev.description: Optional revision to blame against (default: HEAD).

start_line.description: First line to include in blame output.

max_lines.description: Maximum number of lines to include.

name: git_diff
description: Run `git diff` in the workspace with sensible defaults and safe truncation.

path.description: Optional subdirectory or file to scope the diff to (must be within the workspace).

unified.description: Number of context lines to include around changes.

name: git_log
description: Run `git log` in the workspace with optional path and author/date filters.

path.description: Optional subdirectory or file path to scope history to.

max_count.description: Maximum number of commits to return.

author.description: Optional git author filter (same semantics as `git log --author`).

since.description: Optional lower date bound, e.g. '2 weeks ago' or ISO date.

until.description: Optional upper date bound, e.g. 'yesterday' or ISO date.

name: git_show
description: Run `git show` for a specific revision with optional patch and stats.

rev.description: Revision to show (commit SHA, tag, branch, or ref expression).

path.description: Optional subdirectory or file path to scope output.

unified.description: Context lines for patch output when patch=true.

name: git_status
description: Run `git status --porcelain=v1 -b` in the workspace (optionally scoped to a path).

path.description: Optional subdirectory or file to scope the status to (must be within the workspace).

name: github_close_issue
description: Close a GitHub issue only when structured acceptance evidence is present and approved. For pull requests use github_close_pr; do not call PRs issues in user-facing output. Never close merely because the agent is stopping.

name: github_close_pr
description: Close a GitHub pull request only when structured acceptance evidence is present and approved. Use this for PRs instead of github_close_issue so the UI, audit trail, and comments keep PR wording clear.

name: github_comment
description: Post an evidence-backed GitHub issue/PR comment with gh. Requires approval. Use blocker comments for partial work; do not claim closure without evidence.

name: github_issue_context
description: Read GitHub issue context using gh. Read-only: body/comments/labels/state are summarized and large bodies become task artifacts when a durable task is active.

name: github_pr_context
description: Read GitHub PR context using gh: body/comments/reviews/check status/files and optional diff artifact. Read-only; no push/merge/close.

name: grep_files
description: Search for a regex pattern in workspace files. Use this instead of `grep -r`, `rg`, or `find ... -exec grep` in `exec_shell` — pure-Rust, faster, and respects `.gitignore`. Returns matching lines with context (default: 2 lines before/after each match).

pattern.description: Regular expression pattern to search for

path.description: Directory or file to search (relative to workspace, default: .)

include.description: Glob patterns for files to include (e.g., ['*.rs', '*.ts'])

exclude.description: Glob patterns for files to exclude (e.g., ['*.min.js', 'node_modules/*'])

context_lines.description: Number of context lines before and after each match (default: 2)

case_insensitive.description: Whether to perform case-insensitive matching (default: false)

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 100)

name: handle_read
description: Read a bounded projection from a var_handle returned by tools such as RLM sessions or sub-agents. This does not read artifact ids (`art_...`), tool-call ids (`call_...`), SHA refs, or files; use retrieve_tool_result for spilled tool results/artifacts and read_file for workspace files. Provide exactly one projection: `slice` for char/line slices, `range` for one-based line ranges, `count` for metadata counts, or `jsonpath` for a small JSON-path projection. This retrieves from the handle's backing environment instead of asking the parent transcript to hold the full payload.

handle.description: A var_handle object, or a compact `session_id/name` string. Not an `art_...`, `call_...`, SHA, or file path ref.

slice.description: Zero-based half-open slice over chars or lines.

count.description: Return counts for the handle payload.

introspect.description: Return supported projections, size hints, and copy-pasteable examples for this handle.

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to return in this projection. Defaults to 12000; hard-capped at 50000.

name: image_ocr
description: Extract text from an image (PNG, JPEG, or TIFF) via local OCR. On macOS this uses the built-in Vision framework; otherwise it uses local tesseract when available. Use this for screenshots, scanned receipts/whiteboards, image-only PDFs, or any visual that contains text the model needs to read. Returns the extracted text inline; no file is written.

path.description: Path to the image file (relative to workspace or absolute). PNG / JPEG / TIFF supported.

name: list_dir
description: List entries in a directory relative to the workspace. Use this instead of `ls`, `ls -la`, or `find . -maxdepth 1` in `exec_shell` for directory listings.

name: load_skill
description: Load a skill (SKILL.md body + companion file list) into the next turn's context. Use this when the user names a skill or the task clearly matches a skill listed in the system prompt's `## Skills` section. Faster than read_file + list_dir.

name.description: Skill id (the `name` field from the SKILL.md frontmatter, also shown in the `## Skills` listing).

name: note
description: Append a note to the agent notes file for persistent context across sessions.

name: notify
description: Fire a single desktop notification (OSC 9 / terminal bell). Use sparingly — only when a long-running task completes, when a turn was waiting on a remote operation that just finished, or when the user genuinely needs to come back to the terminal. Pass a short `title` and an optional `body`. Do NOT use this for routine progress updates, conversational acknowledgements, or confirmation that the model is alive — that's noise. The user can disable notifications entirely via `[notifications].method = "off"` in `~/.deepseek/config.toml`; when disabled this tool is a silent no-op.

title.description: Short notification title (≤ 80 chars after truncation). Required.

body.description: Optional longer body (≤ 200 chars after truncation).

name: pandoc_convert
description: Convert a document between formats via pandoc. Reads `source_path` (any pandoc-supported input format — pandoc autodetects from extension), converts to `target_format`, and either writes the result to `output_path` (when provided) or returns the converted text inline. Supported targets: markdown, gfm, commonmark, html, rst, latex, docx, odt, epub, plain, asciidoc. Use this instead of shelling out to pandoc via `exec_shell` — no approval prompt for output_path-less reads, structured errors, and a curated format whitelist.

source_path.description: Path to the source document (relative to workspace or absolute). Pandoc autodetects the input format from the file extension.

target_format.description: One of: markdown, gfm, commonmark, html, rst, latex, docx, odt, epub, plain, asciidoc.

output_path.description: Optional path to write the converted document to. When omitted, the converted text is returned inline (text formats only — binary formats like docx/odt/epub require output_path).

name: pr_attempt_list
description: List PR attempts recorded on a durable task.

task_id.description: Task id; defaults to active task.

name: pr_attempt_preflight
description: Run `git apply --check` for a recorded attempt patch. This is a no-mutation preflight; actual apply remains explicit and approval-gated elsewhere.

task_id.description: Task id; defaults to active task.

name: pr_attempt_read
description: Read one recorded PR attempt and its patch artifact reference.

task_id.description: Task id; defaults to active task.

name: pr_attempt_record
description: Capture current git diff as a durable PR work attempt with patch artifact, changed files, and verification notes.

task_id.description: Task to attach to; defaults to active task.

name: project_map
description: Get a high-level map of the project structure, including key files and a tree view.

max_depth.description: Maximum depth for the tree view (default: 3).

name: read_file
description:
Read a UTF-8 file from the workspace. Use this instead of `cat`, `head`, `tail`, or `sed -n '..p'` in `exec_shell` — it's faster, sandbox-aware, and skips the approval prompt. Plain text is returned as-is; PDFs are auto-extracted via the bundled pure-Rust extractor (no Poppler install required). Image screenshots are OCR-extracted when local OCR is available. Cannot read other non-PDF binaries.

For large files, use `start_line` and `max_lines` to read in chunks. By default, returns at most 200 lines (~16KB). If `truncated="true"` in the response, use `next_start_line` to continue reading. For PDFs, use `pages` instead — `start_line`/`max_lines` only apply to text files.

path.description: Path to the file (relative to workspace or absolute)

pages.description: PDF only: page range to extract, e.g. "1-5" or "10". Ignored for non-PDF files.

name: recall_archive
description: Search prior context cycles for content not in your briefing. Use sparingly — frequent recalls mean your briefing was too sparse; refine your next briefing.

query.description: Search query. Tokenized and BM25-scored against archived messages.

cycle.description: Optional: limit to a specific prior cycle number.

max_results.description: Maximum hits to return (default 3, hard-capped at 10).

name: request_user_input
description: Ask the user 1-3 short questions and return their selections.

name: retrieve_tool_result
description: Retrieve a previously spilled large tool result. Accepts a tool_call_id (`call_abc123`), artifact id (`art_call_abc123`), SHA reference (`sha:<64-hex>` or bare 64-hex from `<TOOL_RESULT_REF>`), relative filename (`call_abc123.txt`, `artifacts/art_call_abc123.txt`), or absolute path under ~/.deepseek. Modes: summary, head, tail, lines, query.

ref.description: Tool call id, artifact id (`art_<id>`), SHA ref (`sha:<64-hex>`), spillover filename, or absolute path under ~/.deepseek.

query.description: Case-insensitive substring to search for when mode=query.

lines.description: Line selector for mode=lines, e.g. "10" or "10-40".

line_count.description: Number of lines for head/tail modes. Default 40, hard cap 500.

max_bytes.description: Maximum bytes of excerpt text returned. Default 8192, hard cap 131072.

max_matches.description: Maximum query matches or signal lines returned. Default 20, hard cap 100.

context_lines.description: Extra lines around each query match. Default 1, hard cap 5.

name: revert_turn
description: Roll back the workspace files to the snapshot taken before a recent turn. Use when the user explicitly asks to undo, revert, or roll back the most recent edits. `turn_offset` is 1-based: 1 reverts the most recent turn, 2 reverts the previous one, and so on (max 50). Conversation history is NOT modified — only working-tree files are restored from the side-git snapshot repo.

turn_offset.description: How many turns back to revert (default 1).

name: review
description: Run a structured code review for a file, git diff, or GitHub pull request.

target.description: File path, PR URL, or the literal 'diff'/'staged' for git diff review.

kind.description: Optional explicit target type: file, diff, or pr.

base.description: Optional git base ref when using diff target (e.g. origin/main).

staged.description: Review staged changes when using diff target (default: false).

max_chars.description: Maximum characters to include from the source (default: 200000).

name: rlm_close
description: Close a named RLM context, tear down its Python kernel, and return usage/lifecycle metadata.

name: rlm_configure
description: Configure a named RLM context: output feedback, child query timeout, recursive sub-RLM depth, and explicit session sharing.

name: rlm_eval
description: Run one Python REPL block against a named RLM context. Returns a bounded projection of stdout/stderr plus metadata. If the code calls FINAL/finalize, the final value is stored as a var_handle retrievable with handle_read instead of copied unbounded into the parent context. Batch child helpers require dependency_mode='independent'; use sub_query_sequence or a sequential loop for dependent work.

code.description: Python code to execute. Do not include markdown fences.

name: rlm_open
description: Open a persistent RLM context. Loads `file_path`, `content`, or `url` into a named Python kernel and returns only metadata: name, length, preview, and sha256. Use this for large or unfamiliar inputs so the parent transcript holds a handle, not the body.

name.description: Caller-chosen context name, unique within this parent session. Defaults to a slug from the source.

content.description: Inline content to load. Capped at 200k chars.

url.description: HTTP/HTTPS URL to fetch through fetch_url and load.

session_object.description: Stable symbolic active-session ref from rlm_session_objects, for example session://active/system_prompt or session://active/messages/0.

name: rlm_session_objects
description: List active prompt/history/session symbolic objects as compact cards. Pass one of the returned `id` values to `rlm_open` as `session_object` to inspect it inside an RLM REPL without copying the full prompt or transcript into the parent context.

name: run_tests
description: Run `cargo test` in the workspace root with optional extra arguments.

args.description: Optional extra arguments to pass to `cargo test` (shell-style).

name: task_cancel
description: Cancel a queued or running durable task through TaskManager. Requires approval because it changes work state.

task_id.description: Full task id or unambiguous prefix.

name: task_create
description: Create/enqueue a durable background task through TaskManager. Durable tasks are restart-aware executable work, distinct from sub-agents.

prompt.description: Work prompt for the durable task.

workspace.description: Workspace path; defaults to current workspace.

name: task_gate_run
description: Run an approved verification gate command and return structured evidence. When inside a durable task, the gate result and log artifact are attached to that task.

cwd.description: Optional working directory within the workspace.

name: task_list
description: List recent durable tasks with status, linked thread/turn ids, and concise summaries.

name: task_read
description: Read durable task detail including timeline, checklist, gate evidence, artifacts, and PR attempts.

task_id.description: Full task id or unambiguous prefix.

name: task_shell_start
description: Start a long-running shell command in the background and return a shell task_id immediately. Use task_shell_wait to poll and optionally record gate evidence on the active durable task.

cwd.description: Optional working directory within the workspace.

name: task_shell_wait
description: Poll a background shell task without blocking the agent indefinitely. If `gate` is supplied and the shell task has completed, records structured gate evidence on the active durable task.

task_id.description: Background shell task id returned by task_shell_start or exec_shell.

command.description: Original command, used when recording gate evidence.

name: todo_add
description: Compatibility alias for checklist_add. Adds one checklist item on the active thread/task.

name: todo_list
description: Compatibility alias for checklist_list. Lists current checklist progress.

name: todo_update
description: Compatibility alias for checklist_update. Updates one checklist item by id on the active thread/task.

name: todo_write
description: Compatibility alias for checklist_write. Replace the active thread/task checklist; durable tasks are the real executable work object.

todos.description: The complete list of todo items. This replaces the existing list.

name: tool_agent
description: Open an experimental fast-lane execution agent (Fin): DeepSeek V4 Flash with thinking forced off. Use it for simple tool-bound work such as OCR, file/search lookups, fetches, or command probes where the parent model should keep planning and synthesis context clean. Returns the same session projection as agent_open; use agent_eval to fetch/wait and agent_close to close it. Do not use this for nuanced implementation, architecture, release decisions, or tasks that need careful reasoning.

name.description: Stable model-facing session name. Defaults to the generated agent_id when omitted.

prompt.description: Initial tool-bound task for the fast execution agent

items.description: Structured input items (text, mention, skill, local_image, image)

allowed_tools.description: Optional explicit tool allowlist for this executor

cwd.description: Optional working directory for the child; must be inside the parent workspace

fork_context.description: Defaults to false. Set true only when the executor needs the parent prefix.

max_depth.description: Recursive child-agent budget. Defaults to 0 for tool_agent.

name: update_plan
description: Update optional high-level strategy metadata for complex initiatives. Use checklist_write for primary Work progress; update_plan should capture phase-level approach changes, not duplicate checklist items. Each strategy step has a description and status (pending, in_progress, completed). Optionally include an explanation of the overall approach.

explanation.description: Optional high-level explanation of the plan or approach

name: validate_data
description: Validate JSON or TOML content from inline input or a workspace file.

path.description: Optional path to a file within the workspace.

format.description: Validation format. 'auto' infers from extension then falls back to trying both.

name: web-x00002E-run
description: Browse the web (search/open/click/find/screenshot/image_query) and return structured results with ref_ids for citations.

name: web_search
description: Search the web and return ranked results with URLs and snippets. Default backend is Bing; set `[search] provider = "duckduckgo" | "tavily" | "bocha"` in config.toml to switch backends. Use this instead of scraping search engines with `curl` in `exec_shell`. For a known canonical URL, prefer `fetch_url` directly.

query.description: Search query. Compatibility aliases: q, or search_query[0].q.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 5, max: 10)

name: write_file
description: Write content to a UTF-8 file in the workspace. Use this instead of heredocs (`cat <<EOF > file`) or `echo > file` in `exec_shell` — diffs render inline and approval is handled cleanly. Creates or overwrites; parent directories are auto-created.

name: code_execution
description: Execute Python code in a local sandboxed runtime and return stdout/stderr/return_code as JSON.

name: js_execution
description: Execute JavaScript code in a local sandboxed Node.js runtime and return stdout/stderr/return_code as JSON.

name: tool_search_tool_regex
description: Search deferred tool definitions using a regex query and return matching tool references.

query.description: Regex pattern to search tool names/descriptions/schema.

name: tool_search_tool_bm25
description: Search deferred tool definitions using natural-language matching and return matching tool references.

query.description: Natural language query for tool discovery.
